name: What is Physics?
description: Understanding what physics really is and why it matters

content.overview: Physics is the study of how things work. You observe something happen, you figure out the rule behind it, and that rule lets you predict what happens next. No magic, no mystery — just observation and reasoning.
content.explanation:
Throw a ball. It goes up, then comes down. Physics asks why it comes down, and finds the rule: gravity. Once you know the rule, you can predict exactly where the ball lands before you throw it.

That's the pattern for all of physics: observe something → find the rule behind it → predict what happens next.

It works the same way a detective works a case. You observe the clues (the ball falls), you find the rule (gravity pulls things down), and you use the rule to predict the outcome (where it lands). {{viz:anim-01}}

Once you understand the rules, you can build things with them. Phones, planes, bridges, engines — all of it runs on physics rules someone worked out this same way.

The process breaks into four steps:
- **Observe**: a ball falls, light bounces, things move
- **Ask why**: what makes the ball fall? what makes light bounce?
- **Find the rule**: gravity, reflection, motion
- **Apply the rule**: build stronger structures, faster vehicles, better technology {{viz:chart-01}}

That loop — observe, ask, understand, predict — is physics.
keyPoints: Physics is just figuring out how and why things work
It starts simple: why does a ball fall? How fast does it go?
Then it gets bigger: how do planets orbit? How do atoms work?
Physics is everywhere: your phone, your food, your body
content.whyItMatters: Physics is running in the background of everything you do. It decides how you land when you jump, how light reaches your eyes, how your phone charges. Understanding physics means understanding how the world around you actually works.
